memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< CalibrationSample > &samples, const GazeVector2 &screen_size_mm, const GazeVector3 &initial_camera_offset, double initial_camera_tilt_deg, bool freeze_camera_params, const double params[6], const CalibrationWeights &weights)
memberdef.name: compute_loss

memberdef.argsstring: (bool is_error, std::stringstream &ss, const K &key, const V &val, const Args &... args)
memberdef.name: log_kv_impl

memberdef.argsstring: (int level, const std::string &event, const Args &... args)
memberdef.name: log_info

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint8_t *src, int src_w, int src_h, uint8_t *dst, int dst_w, int dst_h)
memberdef.name: resize_bgr_to_rgb

memberdef.argsstring: (double fov_degrees, double card_distance_mm, double frame_width, double card_width_mm=85.603)
memberdef.name: get_card_width_px

briefdescription.para: Converts a 3D rotation vector (Rodrigues vector) to a 3x3 rotation basis.

detaileddescription.para: Commonly known in graphics/game math as the Axis-Angle to Matrix conversion (where the vector's direction is the axis of rotation and its magnitude is the angle in radians). Named "rodrigues" to match the OpenCV cv::Rodrigues convention since it is used to parse head pose rotations returned by OpenCV's estimator.

briefdescription.para: Converts a 3x3 rotation basis to a 3D rotation vector (Rodrigues vector).

detaileddescription.para: Inverse of rodrigues_to_basis, matching OpenCV's cv::Rodrigues convention.

memberdef.argsstring: (const GazeVector3 &ray_origin, const GazeVector3 &ray_dir, const GazeVector3 &plane_normal, double plane_d, double &out_t)
memberdef.name: intersect_ray_plane

briefdescription.para: Intersects a 3D ray with a plane in 3D space.

detaileddescription.para: Solve for parameter t: plane_normal.dot(ray_origin + t * ray_dir) = plane_d => t = (plane_d - plane_normal.dot(ray_origin)) / plane_normal.dot(ray_dir)

briefdescription.para: Applies pitch and yaw bias corrections (and scaling) to a raw 3D gaze vector.

memberdef.argsstring: (const GazeVector3 &origin_cam, const GazeVector3 &dir_cam, const GazeVector3 &camera_offset, double camera_tilt_deg, const GazeVector2 &screen_size_mm, GazeVector2 &out_pos_mm)
memberdef.name: project_ray_to_screen_mm

briefdescription.para: Projects a camera-space gaze ray onto a tilted screen plane in millimeters.

memberdef.argsstring: (const GazeVector3 &P, const GazeVector3 &r, const GazeVector3 &t, double fx, double fy, double cx, double cy)
memberdef.name: project_point

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< GazeVector3 > &model_points, const std::vector< GazeVector2 > &image_points, const double beta[6], double fx, double fy, double cx, double cy, double residuals[10])
memberdef.name: compute_residuals

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< GazeVector3 > &model_points, const double beta[6], double fx, double fy, double cx, double cy, double J[10][6])
memberdef.name: compute_jacobian

memberdef.argsstring: (double A[6][6], const double b[6], double x[6])
memberdef.name: solve6x6

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< GazeVector3 > &model_points, const std::vector< GazeVector2 > &image_points, double fx, double fy, double cx, double cy, GazeVector3 &rvec, GazeVector3 &tvec, bool use_extrinsic_guess=true)
memberdef.name: solve_pnp_lm

briefdescription.para: Solves the Perspective-n-Point (PnP) problem for 5-point face landmarks using Levenberg-Marquardt optimization.

parameterdescription.para: If true, uses the input values of rvec/tvec as initial guess.

simplesect.para: true if the solver converged successfully, false otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const unsigned char *src, int src_w, int src_h, unsigned char *dst, int dst_w, int dst_h, int pad_x, int pad_y, int new_w, int new_h)
memberdef.name: bilinear_resize_letterbox
